sources_new.author: Peter Meyer (compiler); source reports compiled by 'Pup'
sources_new.work: 340 DMT Trip Reports (attesting to contact with apparently independently-existing intelligent entities)
sources_new.note: Meyer's curated corpus, assembled from anonymous DMT accounts 'Pup' posted to dmt.tribe.net (2005-2006); this is the 'Meyer & Pup' collaboration and the empirical backbone of Meyer's discarnate-entity thesis. Reliability class: community archive of self-reported anonymous accounts, not peer-reviewed; useful for base rates, not for ontology.

sources_new.author: Andrew R. Gallimore
sources_new.work: Alien Information Theory: Psychedelic Drug Technologies and the Cosmic Game
sources_new.note: A computational neuroscientist's book-length speculative framework arguing the DMT breakthrough is perceptual contact with a higher-dimensional, information-encoded reality rather than hallucination. Reliability class: individual theoretical synthesis (grounded in real pharmacology but its cosmological claims are unfalsifiable and explicitly non-consensus).

sources_new.author: Andrew R. Gallimore
sources_new.work: Reality Switch Technologies: Psychedelics as Tools for the Discovery and Exploration of New Worlds
sources_new.note: Gallimore's follow-up detailing how psychedelics interface with cortical dynamics to 'switch' the experienced world; strongest as an accessible account of receptor/brain mechanisms, weaker where it extends to 'genuine other worlds.' Reliability class: individual synthesis mixing peer-reviewed neuroscience with interpretive theory.

sources_new.author: Andrew R. Gallimore & Rick J. Strassman
sources_new.work: A Model for the Application of Target-Controlled Intravenous Infusion for a Prolonged Immersive DMT Psychedelic Experience
sources_new.note: Frontiers in Pharmacology proposal (the intellectual seed of DMTx) arguing DMT's rapid onset and lack of acute tolerance make it amenable to anesthesiology-style target-controlled infusion to sustain the state beyond its ~20 min bolus duration. Reliability class: peer-reviewed pharmacokinetic modeling paper.

sources_new.author: Lisa X. Luan, Emma Eckernäs, Michael Ashton, Christopher Timmermann, David Nutt, Robin Carhart-Harris et al. (Imperial College)
sources_new.work: Psychological and physiological effects of extended DMT (DMTx pilot)
sources_new.note: First empirical realization of the Gallimore-Strassman infusion model: continuous IV DMT sustained peak effects across ~30 min in healthy volunteers with heart rate habituating within ~15 min and low anxiety, establishing basic safety/tolerability. Reliability class: peer-reviewed clinical pilot (small N, placebo-controlled).

sources_new.author: Rick Strassman
sources_new.work: DMT and the Soul of Prophecy: A New Science of Spiritual Revelation in the Hebrew Bible
sources_new.note: Strassman argues that his DMT volunteers' visions closely parallel Hebrew prophetic experience, proposing 'theoneurology' (the divine communicating via endogenous DMT). Reliability class: individual scholarly/theological interpretation by the original clinical DMT researcher; the comparative phenomenology is data, the theological model is interpretive.

sources_new.author: Rick Strassman, Slawek Wojtowicz, Luis Eduardo Luna, Ede Frecska
sources_new.work: Inner Paths to Outer Space: Journeys to Alien Worlds through Psychedelics and Other Spiritual Technologies
sources_new.note: Multi-author sequel to The Spirit Molecule framing DMT 'alien' worlds as inner-space phenomenology; Frecska contributes a serious (if speculative) quantum/biophysics chapter on non-local consciousness. Reliability class: mixed individual accounts + speculative theory from credentialed researchers.

sources_new.author: Graham St John
sources_new.work: The Breakthrough Experience: DMT Hyperspace and Its Liminal Aesthetics
sources_new.note: Peer-reviewed cultural-anthropology companion to St John's cultural history (Mystery School in Hyperspace), analyzing the 'breakthrough' as a liminal aesthetic and social ritual rather than a pharmacological fact. Reliability class: peer-reviewed humanities/anthropology (Anthropology of Consciousness).

sources_new.author: David Luke & Rory Spowers (eds.); Anton Bilton, contributors incl. Strassman, Dennis McKenna, Hancock, Narby, Sheldrake, Metzner
sources_new.work: DMT Dialogues: Encounters with the Spirit Molecule
sources_new.note: Proceedings of the 2017 Tyringham Hall symposium where 20 researchers debated entity encounters, plant sentience, and DMT ontology; the fullest single collection of David Luke's entity-encounter framing. Reliability class: edited scholarly-but-speculative dialogue (mixes peer-reviewed researchers with frankly metaphysical positions).

sources_new.author: Dennis McKenna
sources_new.work: The Brotherhood of the Screaming Abyss: My Life with Terence McKenna
sources_new.note: Memoir/ethnopharmacology by the scientifically-trained McKenna brother (later of the Hoasca ayahuasca study), documenting the 1971 La Chorrera experiment and the origins of the tryptamine mythos; the sober counterpoint to Terence's mythmaking. Reliability class: individual memoir by a credentialed ethnopharmacologist.

sources_new.author: Terence McKenna & Dennis McKenna
sources_new.work: The Invisible Landscape: Mind, Hallucinogens, and the I Ching
sources_new.note: The McKenna brothers' foundational theoretical text linking tryptamine states, shamanism, and their 'Timewave' I Ching model; historically important as origin of the hyperspace/self-transforming-machine-elf lexicon. Reliability class: individual speculative theory (the Timewave is not scientifically supported), primary-source for the DMT subculture's vocabulary.

sources_new.author: Terence McKenna
sources_new.work: Food of the Gods: The Search for the Original Tree of Knowledge
sources_new.note: McKenna's cultural/ethnobotanical history of psychoactive plants and the 'Stoned Ape' origin-of-consciousness hypothesis; influential on the DMT mythos. Reliability class: individual speculative synthesis (Stoned Ape hypothesis is unproven and widely disputed), valuable as cultural primary source.

sources_new.author: Luis Eduardo Luna & Pablo Amaringo
sources_new.work: Ayahuasca Visions: The Religious Iconography of a Peruvian Shaman
sources_new.note: Ethnographic catalogue pairing Amaringo's painted ayahuasca visions with Luna's anthropological commentary; a primary iconographic record of vegetalista visionary content (entities, cities, serpents). Reliability class: scholarly ethnography + individual visionary art.

sources_new.author: Luis Eduardo Luna
sources_new.work: Vegetalismo: Shamanism among the Mestizo Population of the Peruvian Amazon
sources_new.note: Luna's academic doctoral ethnography documenting mestizo ayahuasquero practice and the concept of 'plant teachers' (doctores); foundational anthropological grounding for entity/teacher motifs. Reliability class: peer-adjacent academic ethnography.

sources_new.author: Ralph Metzner (ed.)
sources_new.work: Sacred Vine of Spirits: Ayahuasca (rev. ed. of Ayahuasca: Human Consciousness and the Spirits of Nature)
sources_new.note: Metzner's edited anthology of ayahuasca science, phenomenology, and first-person accounts, with attention to recurring visionary content; bridges DMT pharmacology and ceremonial context. Reliability class: edited scholarly + experiential accounts.

sources_new.author: Benny Shanon
sources_new.work: Ayahuasca Visualizations: A Structural Typology
sources_new.note: Shanon's peer-reviewed structural typology of ayahuasca visual content (Journal of Consciousness Studies), a rigorous phenomenological taxonomy predating and complementing his book The Antipodes of the Mind. Reliability class: peer-reviewed first-person phenomenology (large personal + informant corpus).

sources_new.author: Camila Sanz, Federico Zamberlan, Earth & Fire Erowid, Enzo Tagliazucchi
sources_new.work: The Experience Elicited by Hallucinogens Presents the Highest Similarity to Dreaming within a Large Database of Psychoactive Substance Reports
sources_new.note: NLP analysis of ~15,000 Erowid Experience Vault reports finding hallucinogen (incl. DMT/ayahuasca) narratives are semantically closest to dream reports. Reliability class: peer-reviewed computational corpus study (self-report data, but quantitative and reproducible).

sources_new.author: Federico Zamberlan, Camila Sanz, R. Martínez Vivot, C. Pallavicini, F. Erowid, E. Erowid, E. Tagliazucchi
sources_new.work: The Varieties of the Psychedelic Experience: A Study of the Association Between Subjective Effects and Binding Affinity Profiles of Tryptamines and Phenethylamines
sources_new.note: Latent-semantic-analysis of Erowid reports mapping subjective-effect language onto receptor binding-affinity profiles; quantifies which DMT-family effects track 5-HT2A vs other receptors. Reliability class: peer-reviewed computational corpus study.

sources_new.author: Andrea Alamia, Christopher Timmermann, David Nutt, Rufin VanRullen, Robin Carhart-Harris
sources_new.work: DMT Alters Cortical Travelling Waves
sources_new.note: eLife EEG study (task brief dated it 2023; actual publication is 2020) showing DMT suppresses top-down alpha travelling waves and boosts bottom-up forward waves plus signal diversity, supporting the 'reduced precision of priors' model of the DMT world-building. Reliability class: peer-reviewed neuroimaging.

sources_new.author: Pascal Michael, David Luke, Oliver Robinson
sources_new.work: An Encounter with the Self: A Thematic and Content Analysis of the DMT Experience from a Naturalistic Field Study
sources_new.note: The 'middle' Michael/Luke/Robinson field-study paper (companion to 'An Encounter with the Other', 2021), analyzing self- and emotion-related themes in observed real-world DMT sessions (vaporized 40-75 mg). Reliability class: peer-reviewed qualitative field study (naturalistic, non-blinded).

sources_new.author: Josie Kins (and contributors)
sources_new.work: Effect Index / Subjective Effect Index (incl. Autonomous Entity, geometry & DMT effect taxonomy)
sources_new.note: A community-built granular taxonomy of 200+ subjective psychedelic effects with definitions, subcomponents, and visual replications, including formal 'autonomous entity' and geometry classifications applied to DMT. Reliability class: structured community archive/taxonomy (systematic but not peer-reviewed).

sources_new.author: DMT-Nexus community
sources_new.work: Hyperspace Lexicon (DMT-Nexus Wiki)
sources_new.note: Crowd-built glossary formalizing the shared vocabulary and entity classifications of the DMT subculture (Chrysanthemum, Waiting Room, the entity taxonomy); distinct from the DMT-Nexus forum itself as a curated reference wiki. Reliability class: community lexicon (documents folk-phenomenological consensus, not evidence for it).

sources_new.author: James L. Kent
sources_new.work: Psychedelic Information Theory: Shamanism in the Age of Reason
sources_new.note: The essential skeptical counterweight: Kent argues DMT 'entities' are concrete, fully-immersive hallucinations generated by destabilized/oscillating perceptual control systems, NOT contact with autonomous beings. Reliability class: individual skeptical/neurological synthesis (20+ years, 200+ references; interpretive, not experimental).

sources_new.author: Peter Sjöstedt-Hughes
sources_new.work: Noumenautics: Metaphysics – Meta-ethics – Psychedelics (and co-ed., Philosophy and Psychedelics, 2022)
sources_new.note: Academic philosopher (Whitehead/Spinoza/panpsychism) supplying rigorous ontological frameworks for interpreting psychedelic and 5-MeO-DMT unitive states without collapsing into literalism. Reliability class: peer-adjacent academic philosophy (argues about what the experiences could mean, makes no empirical claims).

sources_new.author: Christopher M. Bache
sources_new.work: LSD and the Mind of the Universe: Diamonds from Heaven
sources_new.note: ADJACENT (high-dose LSD, not DMT): a religious-studies professor's systematic first-person record of 73 high-dose (500-600 µg) LSD sessions over 20 years, included for comparative cosmological/entity phenomenology. Reliability class: single detailed individual account, explicitly cautionary.

sources_new.author: W. Y. Evans-Wentz
sources_new.work: The Fairy-Faith in Celtic Countries
sources_new.note: Classic folklore ethnography of Celtic fairy belief (changelings, abductions, the 'good people'); a pre-psychedelic cultural precedent for the entity-encounter/abduction motif, cited as parallel by Luke, Hancock, and Vallee. Reliability class: historical folklore ethnography (cultural comparison, not evidence).

sources_new.author: Jacques Vallee
sources_new.work: Passport to Magonia: From Folklore to Flying Saucers
sources_new.note: Argues fairy lore, religious apparitions, and UFO/abduction reports share a common structure across cultures and eras; the standard reference for the 'entity encounters recur trans-culturally' comparison in DMT discourse. Reliability class: individual comparative folklore/anomalistics (pattern-finding, not causal claims).

sources_new.author: Steven A. Barker
sources_new.work: N,N-Dimethyltryptamine (DMT), an Endogenous Hallucinogen: Past, Present, and Future Research / 'facts and myths'
sources_new.note: The authoritative sober review of endogenous DMT: confirms DMT is naturally synthesized in mammals (INMT + AADC) and present in body fluids, but debunks the 'pineal-only / massive endogenous dose' folklore. Reliability class: peer-reviewed pharmacology review by the leading endogenous-DMT analytical chemist.

sources_new.author: Jon G. Dean, Jimo Borjigin et al.
sources_new.work: Biosynthesis and Extracellular Concentrations of N,N-Dimethyltryptamine (DMT) in Mammalian Brain
sources_new.note: Scientific Reports study showing rat cerebral cortex releases DMT at extracellular concentrations comparable to serotonin and other monoamines, with a further rise after experimental cardiac arrest. Reliability class: peer-reviewed neuroscience (rodent; human relevance and function remain open).

sources_new.author: Roland R. Griffiths, Ethan S. Hurwitz, Alan K. Davis, Matthew W. Johnson, Robert Jesse
sources_new.work: Survey of Subjective 'God Encounter Experiences': Comparisons Among Naturally Occurring Experiences and Those Occasioned by Psilocybin, LSD, Ayahuasca, or DMT
sources_new.note: Johns Hopkins PLOS ONE survey (4,285 respondents across 5 groups) comparing non-drug vs psychedelic 'encounter with God/Ultimate Reality' experiences; the key comparison anchor for DMT entity-encounter surveys. Reliability class: peer-reviewed large retrospective survey (self-selected, retrospective).

sources_new.author: Marc Wittmann
sources_new.work: Altered States of Consciousness: Experiences Out of Time and Self
sources_new.note: MIT Press synthesis by a leading time-perception researcher explaining the 'timelessness' and ego-dissolution common to intense DMT/psychedelic states via insular-cortex mechanisms of time and body perception. Reliability class: peer-reviewed-adjacent scholarly synthesis grounded in experimental psychophysics.

data_points.claim: In the Meyer/Pup corpus, roughly two-thirds of DMT accounts explicitly report interacting entities
data_points.detail: A report was tagged 'entities' only if it described one or more apparently independently-existing beings interacting intelligently/intentionally with the observer; near-universal mention of intense color and fractal geometry across the set.

data_points.claim: Naturalistic-observation DMT reports almost universally describe emerging into 'other worlds' and very frequently 'beings'
data_points.stat_or_value: 94% contained encounters with beings; 100% described emerging into other worlds
data_points.detail: Michael, Luke & Robinson 'An Encounter With the Other' (2021), thematic/content analysis of observed real-world DMT sessions.

data_points.claim: Vaporized DMT doses used in the Michael/Luke/Robinson naturalistic field study
data_points.detail: Doses self-administered by participants in settings of their choice; 'An Encounter with the Self' analyzed self- and emotion-related themes.

data_points.claim: Content analysis of online DMT trip reports for entity descriptions
data_points.stat_or_value: 149 reports (90% male, mean age 25), 180 total entity encounters, 2009-2019
data_points.detail: Lyke's descriptive taxonomy of entity appearance/behavior drawn from published online accounts.

data_points.claim: Large international survey of DMT breakthrough entity encounters
data_points.stat_or_value: 2,561 respondents reporting an 'encounter' after a breakthrough inhaled DMT dose
data_points.detail: Davis et al. (2020) — most respondents rated the encounter among the most meaningful experiences of their lives and many believed the entity continued to exist after.

data_points.claim: Following a subjective 'God encounter' experience, self-reported atheism dropped sharply
data_points.stat_or_value: atheism fell from 21% to 8% of the sample after the experience
data_points.detail: 4,285 respondents across 5 groups (non-drug 809, psilocybin 1,184, LSD 1,251, ayahuasca 435, DMT 606); non-drug group picked 'God' as best descriptor while psychedelic groups picked 'Ultimate Reality'.

data_points.claim: A single bolus of DMT produces effects too short to study or work with therapeutically, motivating infusion
data_points.detail: Gallimore & Strassman proposed target-controlled IV infusion (borrowed from anesthesiology) to hold brain DMT steady and prolong the immersive state.

data_points.claim: Extended-state DMT (DMTx) infusion was safe and physiologically stable in the first pilot
data_points.stat_or_value: 28 healthy volunteers; ~30 min continuous IV; heart rate habituated within ~15 min; anxiety stayed low
data_points.detail: Imperial College pilot (single-blind, placebo-controlled, five dose levels), 256-channel EEG + plasma DMT measured; peak effects sustained across the infusion.

data_points.claim: DMT reshapes the brain's dominant travelling-wave direction
data_points.stat_or_value: decreased alpha/beta (13-30 Hz) power; top-down alpha waves down, bottom-up forward waves up; increased signal diversity
data_points.detail: First EEG study of pure DMT on ongoing activity; supports 'reduced precision-weighting of priors' as a mechanism for the vividness of the DMT world.

data_points.claim: Endogenous DMT is released in mammalian brain at neurotransmitter-scale concentrations
data_points.stat_or_value: rat cortical extracellular DMT comparable to serotonin/monoamines; rises further after cardiac arrest
data_points.detail: Present with or without the pineal gland, undercutting 'pineal-only' claims; function in humans remains unestablished.

data_points.claim: The 'pineal gland floods you with DMT' story is folklore; DMT synthesis is real but modest and not pineal-exclusive
data_points.stat_or_value: INMT + AADC required; INMT transcripts found in cortex, pineal, and choroid plexus
data_points.detail: Barker confirms DMT is a genuine endogenous compound detectable in blood/urine/CSF but rebuts the myth of large pineal DMT surges at birth/death.

data_points.claim: The leading skeptical position holds DMT entities are hallucinations, not autonomous beings
data_points.stat_or_value: framed as 'concrete', fully-immersive hallucinations from destabilized perceptual control
data_points.detail: Kent's Psychedelic Information Theory (20+ years, 200+ references) explicitly denies DMT is a gateway to alternate dimensions or contact with independent elves/aliens — the honesty-charter counterweight.

data_points.claim: A granular community taxonomy formalizes 'autonomous entity' as a defined subjective effect
data_points.detail: Josie Kins' Subjective Effect Index lists autonomous entities among high-level internal hallucinations alongside sceneries, landscapes, and all-encompassing geometry for DMT.

data_points.claim: Hallucinogen experience narratives are, linguistically, closest to dream reports
data_points.stat_or_value: highest semantic similarity to dreaming across a large multi-substance report database
data_points.detail: NLP/latent-semantic analysis of thousands of Erowid Experience Vault reports (DMT/ayahuasca among the hallucinogens compared).

data_points.claim: Comparative (non-DMT) benchmark for sustained high-dose visionary cosmology
data_points.stat_or_value: 73 high-dose LSD sessions (500-600 µg) over 20 years (1979-1999)
data_points.detail: Bache's single-subject record is adjacent (LSD, not DMT) but offered as a comparison case for recurrent entity/cosmic-consciousness phenomenology; author cautions strongly against replication.
